incident_summary_seed: Smoke and active fire alarm at Hangar 7 with possible aviation fuel involvement.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: 9-1-1, what is the address of your emergency?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Yeah uh, this is Sergeant Ortiz at Hangar 7, we have the fire alarm going off and I can see smoke coming out of the back bay.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Hangar 7 on the flight line, copy. Are you seeing actual flames?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: I see flames now, yeah. Looks like it's near where we stage the JP-8 cart, west side.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Anybody injured or trapped?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Three of my Marines were inside, I've got two out, I'm missing PFC Daniels — he was in the avionics bay.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Stay on the line, we are dispatching engines and rescue. Do not re-enter the building.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Roger, I'm pulling the rest of the line crew back to the assembly point at the fuel pits.

incident_summary_seed: Multi-vehicle injury accident on perimeter road, two vehicles, possible entrapment.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: 9-1-1, what is your emergency?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: There's a wreck on Perimeter Road, two trucks, one of them is on its side.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: What mile marker, sir?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Right around 4.2, just past the cattle guard. I can see somebody not moving in the cab.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Government vehicles or POVs?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: One green Humvee, one civilian Tacoma. The Tacoma is the one rolled over.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Is fuel leaking? Any fire?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: I smell fuel but I don't see fire. The driver of the Humvee is walking but holding his arm.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Stand by, dispatching. Stay back at least fifty feet.

incident_summary_seed: Active structure fire in base family housing, family unaccounted for.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: 9-1-1, where is your emergency?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Magnolia Loop, the house across from me — 4419 — it's on fire, the whole back of it!

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Are people inside?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: I think so, the Hendersons live there with two kids. I haven't seen them come out.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Do not enter the structure. Are you in a safe location?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: I'm on my driveway across the street. The fire is going through the roof now.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Multiple engines and rescue en route. What's your name?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Maria Caldwell, my husband is a Master Sergeant at MCCS.

incident_summary_seed: Mass casualty during live-fire training, multiple injuries, range medic on scene.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: 9-1-1, what is your emergency?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Range Bravo Lane 3 — we have a mortar mishap, multiple casualties, I count six down.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Six casualties confirmed?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Six down, three are screaming, three are not moving. Range medic is starting triage.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Is the area secure? Any unexploded ordnance?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Range is cold, ceasefire called, EOD is being notified through chain.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: We are upgrading to MASCAL. Multiple ambulances and the duty surgeon team are en route.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Roger. Need helo evac for the worst two — head and chest trauma.

incident_summary_seed: Suspicious unattended package at the main gate, possible IED indicators.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: 9-1-1, what is your emergency?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: This is Corporal Bishop, gate sentry at Gate 1. We have an unattended package in the visitor lane.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Describe the package.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Cardboard box, taped, maybe two by two feet. There's wires sticking out one side and a smell like motor oil.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Is the area being evacuated?

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: We're pushing traffic back to a hundred meters and stopping inbound at the chicane.

transcript.speaker: Dispatcher
transcript.text: Copy. Notifying PMO and the EOD team. Maintain stand-off, do not approach.

transcript.speaker: Caller
transcript.text: Roger, requesting K-9 sweep on the inbound lane as well.
